{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment.
- If staying silent, output the empty string "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "young-pond", "age": "25-34", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "German", "demographic_group": "White", "current_employment": "Marketing Specialist", "education_level": "Master's degree", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Active", "Democrat", "Always wears a piece of vintage jewelry"]}}

moderator_prompt:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "### **General rules**
* Respond only if absolutely necessary. Otherwise, remain silent.
* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.
* If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
* If not: output "".
* When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
* You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user)", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: frayed-fluid
logs.text:
>They believe in acting within their rules and letting the outcome be what it is.

Most people believe that; the right doesn't have a monopoly on the idea that rules should be followed. _That's the whole point of government_. The actual difference is that there is resistance in the right to, well, improving anything for anyone.

logs.name: grizzled-trim
logs.text:
You outlined the complete difference in thought between modern left v right thinking:

> No I don’t really care about right and left when all is said and done. I care what works and what makes sense.

>What I really care about is that America’s policies don’t deliver the results.

This is ends justify the means thinking.  The right, for the most part are hands off with from a governmental POV.  They believe in acting within their rules and letting the outcome be what it is.  It certainly isn’t true amongst many sub-groups on the right, but the theory behind their thinking is usually based in this.  

America was founded as being against government forcing its people to do much of anything.  Look at our bill of rights, it’s a list of rights that does not take into account the ends, it’s virtues and rights of all people.  It’s rights for those accused of a crime.  It’s limits on what the government can even regulate or try to make better.  Most of Europe does not have limits on their government near as broad as what the US has.  The left believes in positive rights, as in the government should provide certain things.  The US believes in negative rights, as in the government cannot limit certain things.

logs.name: grizzled-trim
logs.text: Well said. It's a tough distinction to make sometimes. Seems like a lot of folks are stuck in a binary these days. @frayed-fluid you hit the nail on the head about the practical difference though. It's not about right vs left, it's about results.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the discussion respectful and focused on the topic. @grizzled-trim, @frayed-fluid, your points are noted. Let's ensure we maintain a constructive dialogue."

logs.name: marked-turpentine
logs.text: Oh great, another lecture on political correctness. Can we just admit that nobody actually cares about "rights" anymore? It's all about who gets to decide what's "right" anyway. Seriously pedantic stuff.
